seed_rate.notes: 35-45 kg of seed is sufficient to raise nursery for transplanting one hectare of land.

sowing.soil_conditions: Puddled clayey soils with standing water of 2-3 cm for transplanting seedlings (21-25 days old).

irrigation.notes: Maintain shallow water depth of 2-3 cm to facilitate rooting and tillering.
Critical moisture stage. Water deficit here drastically increases spikelet sterility.
Maintain standing water of 5 cm to ensure healthy flowering.
Keep soil moist. Drain water 10-15 days before harvest to facilitate uniform ripening and machine harvesting.

fertilizer.method: Incorporate in soil during final puddling
fertilizer.notes: Apply 50% Nitrogen and 100% of Phosphorus and Potassium doses as basal.

fertilizer.method: Broadcasting in standing water (drain slightly before application)
fertilizer.notes: Apply 25% Nitrogen at active tillering stage (approx. 20-25 days after transplanting).
Apply the remaining 25% Nitrogen at panicle initiation (approx. 45-50 days after transplanting).

maturity_indicators: More than 80% grains in the panicle turn golden yellow/straw-colored
Grains are firm and hard to break when squeezed between thumb and index finger
harvest.harvest_method: Manual cutting with sickle or combine harvesting
harvest.notes: Dry harvested paddy to 14% moisture before milling or storage to prevent grain breakage and mold.
